creator: zirias@freebsd.org
text: On my machine with 8GB physical RAM and 8GB swap, I experience problems since the last upgrade from ports as well, but I didn't find the "smoking gun" yet to be sure it's because of chromium. What I observed so far is memory (and swap) usage continuously going up and the whole system stalling more and more frequently (probably because of I/O for swapping). As one of my chromium processes had a size of 11GB, and I couldn't reproduce this behavior so far without starting chromium (but I will do more tests to be sure), I assume it might be part of the issue.
